title: Iterative acylation on mature lasso peptides by widespread acetyltransferases
objective: Discover and characterize GCN5-related N-acetyltransferases (GNATs) that perform iterative acylation on mature lasso peptides, and develop structure-based engineering strategies to create lipolasso peptides
problem_statement: Traditional iterative enzymes in RiPP biosynthesis install post-translational modifications on linear peptides, not mature RiPPs with complex 3D structures. No iterative enzyme had been shown to iteratively modify RiPPs with intricate three-dimensional architectures like lasso peptides. This limitation restricted the chemical diversity of engineered RiPPs
research_novelty: Discovery that GCN5-related N-acetyltransferases (GNATs) can iteratively and consecutively acylate two Lys residues within lasso peptide loop and ring motifs - the first reported case of iterative modification on mature RiPPs with complex 3D structures

lasso_peptide_structure.key_feature: C-terminal linear segment threads through macrolactam ring
lasso_peptide_structure.ring_formation: Isopeptide bond between N-terminal amine and Asp/Glu side chain carboxyl (position 7-9)

structural_components.ring: Macrolactam structure formed by isopeptide bond
structural_components.loop: C-terminal segment positioned at ring opening

lasso_peptide_structure.biosynthetic_complexity: Requires ribosomal precursor translation, recognition elements, trans-glutaminase proteolysis, and macrolactam cyclization

gnat_discovery.initial_identification: BGC from Actinosynnema mirum DSM 43827 (iat BGC - iterative acetyltransferases)
gnat_discovery.sequence_relationship: IatT and AlbT share 54% sequence identity over 93% coverage

class_i.characteristics: Encode core peptide with single conserved Lys10

class_ii.characteristics: Encode core peptide with two Lys residues
class_ii.exceptions: JiaA and AlkA contain Lys5 and Lys10

bgc_components: IatB: RiPP recognition element (RRE) - trans-glutaminase protease fusion

iterative_modification_mechanism.substrate_changes: Significant tuning of lasso peptide-binding poses in substrate-binding pocket
iterative_modification_mechanism.step_1: First acetylation at one Lys position (e.g., Lys4 in loop motif)
iterative_modification_mechanism.step_2: Second acetylation at different Lys position (e.g., Lys10 in ring motif)
iterative_modification_mechanism.key_residues: Defined through cryo-EM and computational analysis to distinguish two distinct acetylation steps
iterative_modification_mechanism.binding_pocket_adaptation: Enzyme adapts binding geometry to accommodate different Lys positions

structural_findings: Identified key residues for two-step acetylation

rational_engineering.strategy: Structure-based redesign to accommodate longer-chain acyl groups
outcomes: Production of monoacetylated lasso peptides with longer chain length
Production of diacylated lasso peptides with longer chain length

lipolasso_peptides.structure: Lasso peptides with iteratively installed longer-chain acyl modifications
lipolasso_peptides.creation_method: Rational engineering of IatT acetyl-recognition site
lipolasso_peptides.significance: New-to-nature ribosomal lipopeptides with potential enhanced bioactivity

generalizability.strategy: Structure-based engineering approach applicable to any RiPP biosynthetic gene cluster encoding GNAT
generalizability.expected_outcome: Efficient diversification of ribosomal lipopeptides from diverse RiPP families
generalizability.broader_impact: Enables systematic generation of lipidated RiPP variants

catalytic_diversity.ripp_context: GNATs represent iterative biocatalysts that expand catalytic diversity in RiPP biosynthesis
catalytic_diversity.novelty: First demonstration of iterative catalysis on mature RiPPs with complex 3D structures
catalytic_diversity.previous_paradigm: Iterative modifications traditionally occurred on linear peptides

research_impact.fundamental_discovery: Challenges paradigm that iterative PTM enzymes modify only linear peptides
research_impact.structural_biology: Reveals how enzymes adapt binding poses for different substrate positions
research_impact.biotechnology: Provides platform for rational engineering of biocatalysts
research_impact.drug_development: Creates new chemical scaffolds and structural diversity in RiPP library
research_impact.future_directions: Template for discovering additional iterative enzymes on structured RiPPs
